items.speaker: Battier
items.text: A bottle of Troupe Strength, just for you! Around these parts, this stuff is as good as gold.

items.speaker: Battier
items.text: Oh, and look what I found while gutting the fish!

options.text: Let me see.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's… split in half?

items.speaker: Battier
items.text: Odd, right? Found it just like this. It might even be a relic from the old days.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Mmm?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Look, there's something there…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: May I have a look?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Is that a letter? Does it mean anything to you?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hmm, yes. On these seas, there is only one legend about "D."

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: The story of Drake, the Golden Captain.

items.speaker: Tina
items.text: Captain Drake! That adventurer everyone talks about?

items.speaker: Levito
items.text: I thought that was just a fanciful story…

options.text: Who is Captain Drake?
What do they say about him?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Captain Drake, the famed hero of Rinascita. Legend has it he assembled a crew with naught but a single coin and amassed great riches from the wealthiest of men.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: He then embarked on a quest to chart a safe passage across the Stormy Sea. A feat worthy of being remembered forever!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: But there was a price. Only one crew member returned, and he had been driven mad. What happened out there is still a mystery.

options.text: You seem to know the story well.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Such tales were my bedtime stories.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Back when I just boarded the Pilgrim's Sail, with no family and nightmares that could raise the dead, Toscanini would share such stories to comfort me.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: At the time, I swore that if I ever became a captain, every crew member would come back safe. No exceptions.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Ah, Toscanini, the Night Watcher. Hahaha. You're one of the few who still remembers him.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Did he ever tell you the rest? That mad sailor who raved about his captain's coin, saying it would lead to untold treasures.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Perhaps it's the very coin our Captain here is holding.

items.speaker: Battier
items.text: Untold treasures, eh? Well, aren't we lucky. If we had the whole thing, imagine the riches we'd claim...

options.text: Do these treasures really exist?
Any idea where the other half of the coin might be hiding？

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Oh, they exist, alright. The Golden Captain conquered every sea known to man. The wealth he amassed could give us all lives of leisure and luxury.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: You raise a fine point. Where is the other half…

items.speaker: Hymer
items.text: Half a coin… Treasures… Blast!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Something wrong, Hymer?

items.speaker: Hymer
items.text: Now that I think of it… I lost a half-coin just like this not long ago.

options.text: You sure? One like this?

items.speaker: Hymer
items.text: Yes, almost identical! There was a man yapping about its value. I thought he was just bluffing.

items.speaker: Hymer
items.text: Didn't take him seriously... then I lost it to Bella.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Bella… Since the pirates started stirring the waters around Ragunna, she's been hiring us to guard her family ships.

items.speaker: Hymer
items.text: Aye! She's a regular at Trattoria Margherita. Loves their pizzas. I run into her all the time!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Well then, our next stop is clear. Fortune has a way of smiling on us, Captain. Shall we try our luck at the restaurant?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: What say you? Ready to check it out?

options.text: Don't have to ask me twice.
Sailor Brant, lead the way.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Aye, aye, Captain! Our course is set! Ready the sails, and let the wind carry us!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: How was my improvised performance, Cap? No applause or cheers for your humble bard?

options.text: Bravo! Bravo!
I thought you were drunk.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Thank you! Thank you!

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: That was a wonderful performance!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: To be honest, I came dangerously close to it…

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: Take care not to overindulge again. We don't offer home delivery for humans, I assure you.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Alright, seems like we've got ourselves a lucky gentleman.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: How about an introduction first, and then you tell us where that coin is?

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: …Name's Swiper. Go ahead, gloat.

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: Chalk it up to a bit of lousy luck. You lot would never have caught me otherwise.

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: But the coin you're after? It's long gone. I barely had it before pirates backed me into a corner.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Pirates?

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: Yeah, their captain, name's something like Aldric. He took one look at that blasted coin, started muttering something about a midnight rendezvous in ugh... Egla. Whatever they're planning, I don't like the sound of it.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Aldric… so it is him.

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: That's all I know! If you want the coin, go catch him yourself. Now let me go! I'm innocent!

items.speaker: Bella
items.text: Innocent? You stole the coin from me, and now you dare claim innocence?

items.speaker: Margherita
items.text: Despicable! After all you've done to our customers, you show no remorse. You're off to the Order Tribunal for judgment!

items.speaker: Swiper
items.text: The Order Tribunal…? Hey, you can't do this to me! I told you where the coin is! You owe me for that information!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Of course! And your payment will be a one-way trip to the Order Tribunal. May you start a fresh chapter there, my friend.

items.speaker: Bella
items.text: Move! I'm taking you to the Tribunal. You'll be coughing up every single thing you've stolen, and then some!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: An old acquaintance of mine, so to speak. We were banished on the Pilgrim's Sail together. I remember he even had a nickname—Quickfoot Aldric.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: But he always had trouble embracing our ideals, so he left the troupe before long.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: The next time we saw him, he had already become a pirate.

options.text: So he was once part of the Troupe of Fools.
Hard to believe he's chosen to side against the Troupe of Fools.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: He's probably after Drake's treasures as well, which explains why he took the coin from Swiper.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Makes sense. But what's got him heading to Egla Town at midnight?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Well, that's the part we'll need to see for ourselves.

options.text: Then our course is set once again.
Let's go see for ourselves.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Looks like we'll be burning the midnight oil. Once we arrive, we'd better keep an eye out for those plushies.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: KILL THEM!

items.speaker: Pirate Crowd
items.text: Over there!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: All yours, Captain!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Aldric fled like a cat with its tail on fire. Still living up to his old nickname, Quickfoot.

options.text: I would've liked to catch him.
At least we saved those innocent Echoes.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Don't worry, Captain. I've got a feeling our paths will cross again soon enough.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Indeed, and we reclaimed this half-coin. And next time we're in Ragunna, we'll return it to Bella.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I've got a feeling we'll cross paths with Aldric again soon enough.

items.speaker: Unsad Lottie Lost
items.text: Lottie… Lost!

options.text: I can tell she's happier now.
She's saying "Bravo!"

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Well, would you look at that. Captain, I didn't know you were fluent in Echo!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Miss, as promised, you and your friends can now dance in the moonlight to your heart's content. Go on, enjoy!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: It seems Aldric spent too much time on the sea and forgot the fundamental rule of this trade: quid pro quo.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: If you want the sea's rewards, you have to give something in return. Just like how being a captain doesn't just come with rights, but duties too.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Every reward has its price, and tricking his men into paying it for his own gain is absolutely unforgivable.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Anyway, take a look at this.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: This map marks three isles. I'm betting they're the hiding places for Drake's treasure.

options.text: Do you have wishes you want to fulfill?
Are you after Drake's treasures, too?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Haha, well, who could resist the siren call of Drake's fabled riches? It's tempting, no doubt.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: But truth be told, it's not the treasures themselves that draw me in. It's the stories—the legend of Drake himself.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: What did he face? What mysteries did he leave behind? There's no grander performance than that, Captain.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: We've come this far already. I'd regret it forever if I turned back now. So yes, I want to visit those isles, and maybe we can uncover the truth about Drake's treasures.

options.text: But we'll need a ship.
What if we run into more pirates?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Ha, that's exactly what I was thinking.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Pirates? No big deal. We'll deal with them like we always do.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Luckily for us, one of our troupe's Gondolas has been docked since the Carnevale. Looks like it'll come in handy after all.

options.text: Time's ticking. Let's go.
I'm ready to take the wheel this time.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Off we go! I'll lead the way.

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Alright, we yield.

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Didn't expect you to be the Flaming Returned, the legendary pirate who can't die… We don't stand a chance.

options.text: What's this, another new dramatic backstory?
So your secret identity is a notorious pirate?
Any more absurd titles you want to tell me about?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: You think so too, huh? Throwing in random plot twists like this will only confuse the audience.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Aha, so this is my hidden identity!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I'm just as curious as you are. With any luck, it'll be a catchy one for my next performance on stage.

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Stop playing coy! You know what I'm on about! It's a tale every sailor has heard!

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: No one knows when he appeared

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Or when he vanished…

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: He was hunted time and again

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: But slipped through every noose

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: They say he's immortal, for no raging storm—

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Nor crashing wave

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Look at me!

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Not even the sharpest blade can kill him!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's quite the legend you've built yourself

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Haha… Let's see how this plays out

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Strong as hell, that one was

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Had the guts to raid the Pilgrim's Sails again and again

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Rescue missions!

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Anyway

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: He always ruined our plans!

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: Every time we set off to raid those merchant ships

items.speaker: Cocky Pirate
items.text: This fiery bastard would swoop in, flames blazing!

items.speaker: Stammering Pirate
items.text: B-b-beyond outrageous!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Brant. I didn't think you'd show up so soon.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And I didn't think you'd still be here.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hm… where's all that treasure, then? Your crew is itching for the grand return of their selfless, heroic Captain Aldric.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: …Useless fools. Couldn't even slow you down.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Fine, you want the key, don't you? I could give it to you, but—

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Ah, I see! You want an honorable duel with my captain? A proper sea knight's duel, full of valor and—

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: No, no! Must we always resort t-to violence? See that? It takes two keys to unlock!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: You have one, and I have the other.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: We both want the treasures, right? So how about this, I'll give you my key, but we split the treasure. Sixty-forty… No, wait, I-I'll be generous. You take the sixty, and I'll settle for forty.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: You've seen my crew. Those poor souls… I'm doing this for them, to give them the life they deserve. A-a happy, peaceful life.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: What do you say, Captain of the Troupe of Fools? A fair deal, wouldn't you agree?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (He sounds sincere. Can I really trust him?)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Regardless, agreeing to his deal to get ahold of the other key is in my best interest. Even if it's a trap, we can handle it. In that case...)

options.text: (Agree with his proposal.)
(Think about it for a bit longer.)
(Look at Brant.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Deal. Hand over the key.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Come on, we don't have all day! Make a decision, or none of us will get the treasure!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Deal. Hand over the key.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Alright, we accept. Hand over the key.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Here you go. The gate's right over there.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Time to claim our treasures... Wait! What's that!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Ahhhh! You, uh, stay here and defend our great cause! Show them the true might of the Fool's Troupe. FAREWELL!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: The gold… My gold…

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Treasure… My grand cause… No…

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We managed to slip away.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I… I never thought he'd pick those treasures over his own life…

options.text: Are you holding up alright?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Back then… When Toscanini first told us about Drake's story, I said I wanted to be a captain who led the crew to countless triumphs so that everyone gets to carve out their own destiny.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Aldric just laughed, calling it foolish. He said, "You fool! With Drake's treasure, you can crush anyone and make everyone's life better by force!"

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I never bought into that. For me, it's not about riches or power. It's about freedom—the freedom to sail your own course.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: That's why we parted ways. He left the Troupe of Fools and became a pirate.

options.text: The two of you became very different captains.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Amidst all your theatrics, you always remain true to your code.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: As a captain, you're quite different yourself.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Certainly one I'd be proud to follow.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: You accept your crew for who they are, understanding and respecting their choices.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I've had my share of adventures, but I must admit—this one with you has been a breath of fresh air.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: By the way, look at this.

options.text: Is this... Drake's treasure?
What? It looks like an ordinary scroll.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: This is Golden Captain Drake's map, a record of his life.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: According to this, he and his crew did indeed brave the treacherous Stormy Sea and return with immense wealth, but not without paying dearly.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Heavy fog, disease, getting hopelessly lost, each entry could have sunk an entire fleet. By the time they made it back to the isle, only a handful survived.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Most of them never left. In the end, only one sailor remained with this coin.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Remember what that note said? "Remember the law of quid pro quo."

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I believe it means if you want Drake's treasure, you have to offer something in return. The price I paid for this scroll was that coin.

options.text: So, you gave away the coin…
And got this scroll in return?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Yes, but isn't it fascinating? A single coin can lead to both dreadful curses and priceless treasures.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Who would have guessed that Drake's most valuable treasure would be a map worth just one coin?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Honestly, when I made the trade, I wasn't sure what I'd get.

options.text: You were betting on it?
That was quite the gamble.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hahaha! I had faith in you, my friend, and the spirit of adventure.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And this story is far more compelling than the saga of the Flaming Returned, don't you think?

options.text: Didn't expect that title to come up again…
Tell me what all that is really about.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Ah, you remember those pirates who said I was always on the run?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Every time a bounty was placed on my head, I'd work with my crew. They'd turn me in for the reward, and I'd handle the escape.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: The rumors spread faster than I expected. Hahaha…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Anyway, if you're up for it, the trip back is long enough for me to tell you all about it.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: So, Captain, the adventure's come to a close. Ready to sail back home?

options.text: Let's go!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Ah, my dear friends! What a joy to see you both in one piece!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: I-I was absolutely quaking back there. Just the thought of that <ano=Chop Chop>monster</ano>… it still gives me the shivers!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: When I finally came to my senses, I found myself here, all alone. Quite... quite unsettling, I must say.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: But look at you! You took down that beast faster than I could blink.

options.text: Still working on that "grand cause" of yours?

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Grand…? Oh yes! My grand cause! I'm this close to realizing it, you see. Look! Drake's treasure is right there, so brilliant, so-so dazzling…

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: But as a man of principle, I could never hoard such wealth all to myself.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: So, in the spirit of fairness, I'd like to give you the honor of opening it first. Please go ahead, the first pick is yours!

options.text: Leave your bluster at the door.
You're just afraid of the curse, aren't you?

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: C-curse? Hahaha! Do I look like the kind of man who fears mere legends? No, no, my offer comes straight from the heart.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: It's okay.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: In accordance with the law of the sea…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: This is just a teeny tiny curse. Nothing more, nothing less.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: You... you look fine…

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Hahahaha! Curse? Turns out it's as harmless as a kitten!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: I fretted for nothing! Yes, now all this treasure is MINE!

options.text: What about your grand cause?
What about your crew?

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Grand cause? My crew? Oh right…

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: I'll toss a modest pouch to each of my loyal mateys, and the rest? ALL MINE! Hahaha!

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: When I left the Pilgrim's Sail, there was only one thing that stayed with me—to chase my desires without chains.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: And after all these years, I've cracked the code… Money! Hahaha! That's my one true calling. Hahaha!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: But everything comes with a price, Aldric.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And now, it's time to pay yours.

items.speaker: Aldric
items.text: Wh-what?!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Watch out, Captain!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Captain, look over there…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Looks like they're preparing for a grand gathering.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: As much as I'd love to go over and stoke the fires of their fun...

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Those pirates are on high alert. If our approach is clumsy, we may as well shout "Hello! We're here to ruin your party!"

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Agreed. We need to gather intel without being spotted. Let's find a way to sneak past.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Exactly what I was thinking. See those Echoes over there?

options.text: Do you have a plan?
Why do I feel you're about to do something stupid…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Ha, as the saying goes, "At sea, abide by the waves; on land, respect the earth."

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: This is a <ano=Echoes>plushie</ano> gathering, so we'd better fit in with their customs!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Come on, Captain! The night has cast its spell, and it's time for the plushies to dance.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Let's welcome the superstars of tonight's Plushie Gathering—Cuddle Wuddle and Lottie Lost!

options.text: Hi, I'm Cuddle Wuddle.
Wait? Why are you a Lottie Lost?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And I'm Lottie Lost.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Because a troupe can only have one Cuddle Wuddle.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Alright, let's go give Aldric a surprise he won't forget!

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Now you know why we chose to surrender…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Oh, right. I am the Flaming Returned, the immortal scourge of the seas. Cross me, and you'll be haunted by the crackle of flames every night!

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Gold, for the love of gold, don't! I'll spill everything!

items.speaker: Stammering Pirate
items.text: B-but, wouldn't that be betraying our captain?

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Fool! Didn't you hear the captain? "If anyone dares to get in our way, make 'em regret it!"

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: If we don't give 'em what they want, Captain won't sleep a wink. He'll be plagued with nightmares of this menace!

items.speaker: Uncocky Pirate
items.text: Besides, Captain's already off with the key we found. He promised to share the loot with us, and soon I'll be swimming in barrels of wine!

items.speaker: Stammering Pirate
items.text: A-and I can get a gold tooth...

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Oh, what a generous captain you serve… truly selfless.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And yet somehow, these two were left behind to stop us?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Well then, let's head to the next isle.
